LOGIN.INTRO_1: A platform for cybersecurity awareness and assessment.
LOGIN.INTRO_2: Restricted access. Your credentials are transmitted securely.
LOGIN.FORM_SUB: Sign in to access your assessments, training, and risk profile.
LOGIN.SECURITY_NOTE: Never share your credentials. If you are unsure about an email or login link, contact your security team immediately.

HOME.HERO_P: The intelligent platform to analyze human risk, generate tailored quizzes, and deliver targeted training with AI in cybersecurity.
HOME.F1_P: Identify threats related to role, handled assets, and each user's profile.
HOME.F2_P: Deliver assessments aligned with risk level and the most critical security techniques.
HOME.F3_P: Automatically generate learning content adapted to each user's gaps.
HOME.F4_P: Reassess knowledge and progressively reduce risk to your target level.
HOME.HIGHLIGHT_P: CyberForm is not generic content. It links threat techniques, sensitive assets, and user performance for targeted remediation.
HOME.WHY1_P: CyberForm is not generic training. It relies on assets, threats, and MITRE ATT&CK techniques.
HOME.WHY2_P: AI adjusts quizzes and training content based on real performance and needs.
HOME.WHY3_P: Managers get a global view of users, risks, training, and progress.

HOME.CTA_P: Run an analysis, generate tailored quizzes, and track user progress with CyberForm.

ABOUT.TITLE: About CyberForm
ABOUT.P1: CyberForm is a smart learning platform built to help organizations with their cybersecurity strategy.
ABOUT.P2: We help organizations train their people continuously to build resilience against attacks, human error, and digital risks.
ABOUT.P3: With AI, each user receives training adapted to their role and risk level.
ABOUT.P4: The platform is updated regularly with the latest threats and industry practices.

CONTACT.TITLE: Contact the CyberForm team
CONTACT.INTRO: Questions about the platform, an incident to report, or a specific need? Fill in this form and we will get back to you as soon as possible.
CONTACT.NAME: Full name
CONTACT.MESSAGE_PH: Describe your request or issue in detail.
CONTACT.THANKS: Thank you. A team member will respond shortly.
CONTACT.ERR_SEND: Unable to send the message. Please try again later.

PRIVACY.TITLE: Privacy policy
PRIVACY.INTRO: This policy explains how CyberForm processes personal data in the context of the cybersecurity awareness platform. It is designed to align with GDPR principles, provided the application is properly integrated into your environment.
PRIVACY.LI_PROFILE: Profile information: name, work email, role, experience, qualifications.
PRIVACY.PU1: Adapt awareness content to the user's profile and level.
PRIVACY.PU2: Assess changes in individual and collective risk.
PRIVACY.P_SHARE: Data is not sold to third parties. It may be accessed by internal administrators in your organization with a legitimate role (HR, security, training), within their duties.
PRIVACY.P_RETENTION: Data is kept while the user account is active and the platform is used by your organization. Exact retention should follow your company's internal policy.
PRIVACY.P_RIGHTS: Under GDPR, you may exercise access, rectification, or erasure by contacting your organization's platform administrator or: support@cyberform.com.

SECURITY_PAGE.TITLE: Security policy
SECURITY_PAGE.INTRO: At CyberForm, protecting your data is a priority. This page describes measures implemented in the application and practices we recommend.
SECURITY_PAGE.L1_P: Access is restricted to registered users. Each user signs in with a work email and password. This prevents unauthorized access. Checks help reduce suspicious logins. Use strong, unique passwords and rotate them regularly.
SECURITY_PAGE.L2_P: Deploy the platform behind HTTPS to encrypt traffic between browser and server (up-to-date TLS certificates). This reduces interception risk on the network.
SECURITY_PAGE.L3_P: The server logs important technical events (errors, API calls, critical actions) to support investigation after incidents or outages.
SECURITY_PAGE.L4_P: Data is stored in a dedicated database. System administrators should run regular, tested backups and documented restore procedures.
SECURITY_PAGE.L5_P: Users remain responsible for credential confidentiality. Do not share accounts, reuse weak passwords, or sign in from untrusted public devices.
SECURITY_PAGE.OUTRO: This policy describes a baseline suitable for an awareness application. Production deployments should be complemented by your organization's policies (access, backups, monitoring, incident response, etc.).

EVAL.POST_BANNER_TEXT: You must complete the post-training test to validate the training.
EVAL.TODAY_TEXT: An evaluation is scheduled for today. Please complete the cybersecurity test.

ADMIN.SUBTITLE: Overview of your team's cybersecurity posture.
ADMIN.COLLABORATORS_SUBTITLE: Track risk, assessments, and training per user.
ADMIN.SEARCH_PLACEHOLDER: Search by last name, first name, job title, or ID...
ADMIN.ORG_SETTINGS_SUB: Thresholds used when evaluating quizzes (objective reached, normalized risk, critical-technique rule). Changes apply to the next assessments.
ADMIN.ORG_POLICY_PASS_EXPLAIN: Test pass (global): the policy threshold below applies to normalized quiz risk, together with the learned threshold (history) and k. This is separate from the critical-technique vulnerability cap defined further down.
ADMIN.ORG_CARD_GLOBAL_TITLE: 2. Global test objective (normalized risk & learned threshold)
ADMIN.ORG_CRITICAL_FILL_LEAD: Fill in: list mode, optional custom IDs, then the vulnerability cap (%).
ADMIN.ORG_CRITICAL_HELP_SUMMARY: Reminder — evaluation, table below, and training
ADMIN.ORG_CRITICAL_BLOCK_INTRO: Define which MITRE IDs are critical for your organization, then the vulnerability cap (%) that applies to those techniques.
ADMIN.ORG_CRITICAL_BLOCK_L1: List mode: CyberForm built-in, custom (field below), or disabled.
ADMIN.ORG_CRITICAL_BLOCK_L2: For each covered technique, if measured quiz vulnerability exceeds the cap (%), the objective may fail.
ADMIN.ORG_CRITICAL_BLOCK_L3: Per-technique thresholds in the MITRE table below override this cap when set for a row.
ADMIN.ORG_CRITICAL_BLOCK_L4: Personalized training reuses the same list for strict mode.
ADMIN.ORG_CRITICAL_VULN_AFTER_LIST: This percentage is the vulnerability cap for techniques covered by the “critical” rule above (unless overridden per row in the catalog).
ADMIN.ORG_POLICY_THRESHOLD: Policy threshold (%) — normalized risk must stay strictly below
ADMIN.ORG_CRITICAL_CUSTOM: Custom list (one ID per line, e.g. T1566)
ADMIN.ORG_PER_TECH_SUB: For each ID, set the maximum acceptable vulnerability (%). These override the generic “critical techniques” threshold when both apply.
ADMIN.ORG_PER_TECH_PURPOSE: On each assessment, for every MITRE technique present in the quiz, CyberForm computes a vulnerability % (error rate). If you set a “max vuln.” for that technique, the learner’s vulnerability must stay strictly below it for that row to be acceptable; otherwise the objective may fail (similar to the critical-technique rule).
ADMIN.ORG_PER_TECH_CATALOG_HINT: The table lists techniques from the CyberForm catalog. Enter a max % to enable a rule for that technique (empty = no dedicated rule). Values are saved with the “Save” button at the bottom of this block.
ADMIN.MITRE_CATALOG_FILTER_HELP: This field only narrows visible rows (ID or name). It does not save anything: set thresholds in the “Max vuln. (%)” column.
ADMIN.MITRE_CATALOG_EMPTY: No catalog entries (API unavailable or empty list). The search box and table are hidden; use “Other techniques” to enter IDs manually, or check the admin API and backend.
ADMIN.MITRE_CUSTOM_HINT: For an ID not listed above, add a row (sub-technique or out-of-catalog ID).
ADMIN.COMPARISON_SUB: See how scores are distributed to spot groups that need support.
ADMIN.STATS_SUB: Monthly trends for assessments, training, and risk levels.

CREERUSER.TITLE: Create a new collaborator
CREERUSER.PASSWORD_HELP: At least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.
CREERUSER.JOB_PH: e.g. SOC analyst, IT manager, accountant…
CREERUSER.QUALIFICATIONS_PH: One qualification per line (e.g. Master's in Networks, CCNA, 5 years network experience…)
CREERUSER.RISK_INFO: The CIA triad (confidentiality, integrity, availability) will be calculated automatically from assets and role (jobRole). You may still set an initial risk score if needed.
CREERUSER.ERR_PASSWORD_MISMATCH: The two passwords do not match.
CREERUSER.ERR_PASSWORD_POLICY: The password must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.

DETAILUSER_PAGE.TITLE: User details (manager view)
DETAILUSER_PAGE.TRAINING_HISTORY_DESC: The trainings below were generated and sent to the user.
DETAILUSER_PAGE.PER_TECH_VULN_SUB: Vulnerability vs organization thresholds (custom admin threshold or “critical techniques” rule).

QUIZ.NO_ASSETS: No assets linked to your role yet.
QUIZ.TH_T_HELP: T: threat probability factor (MITRE / local risk model). Value between 0 and 1.
QUIZ.SUBTITLE: Answer each question then submit to see the correction.
QUIZ.QUALITY_BELOW_TITLE: The best score remains below the target threshold (see server env QUIZ_MIN_QUALITY_SCORE).
QUIZ.QUALITY_AUTO_TITLE: Server heuristic (lengths, MITRE diversity, Bloom coverage)
QUIZ.ALIGN_NO_FORMATION: No technique list from training (reload after generation, or open training from the quiz flow).
QUIZ.ALIGN_OK: All techniques targeted by the latest training appear in this post quiz.
QUIZ.ALIGN_MISSING: Techniques taught but missing from this quiz:
QUIZ.PER_TECH_VULN_SUB: Vulnerability on this quiz vs admin threshold or “critical techniques” rule.
QUIZ.CRITICAL_TRAINING_DESC: A critical human technique (e.g. social engineering, phishing) exceeds the vulnerability threshold. Targeted training is recommended even if overall risk stays below the organizational threshold.
QUIZ.CMP_RISK_BELOW: Normalized risk is below the final threshold.
QUIZ.CMP_RISK_ABOVE: Normalized risk exceeds the final threshold.
QUIZ.CMP_RISK_EQUAL: Normalized risk equals the final threshold.
QUIZ.ALERT_PRE_DONE: You have already completed the pre-training quiz.
QUIZ.ERR_NO_ASSETS_USER: No assets found for this user.
QUIZ.WARN_PROFILE_PARTIAL: Profile update failed. Showing last saved version.
QUIZ.WARN_PROFILE_REGEN: Regeneration failed. Showing last saved profile.
QUIZ.WARN_PROFILE_FAIL: Unable to load risk profile. Check that the backend is running (port 5001) and server logs.
QUIZ.ERR_QUIZ_EMPTY: Server response had no questions. Retry or check backend logs.
QUIZ.ALERT_TRAINING_SAVED: A new training has been assigned and saved.
